title: Eliminate npm usage

CI-path npm version hotfixed (#96). rg confirms zero npm/npx in those surfaces. Auto-merge armed on #97/#96.

Long-tail prose + legit refs (bun bootstrap, npm-audit reporting, pnpm-install option, slop rule, acp-mux) intentionally excluded per scope. proposal.approval_evidence: User approved (fast-track). One cross-repo cleanup, no guard. web npm/npx→bun + pokeedge 2 dev scripts→bun; CI-path already hotfixed (#96). Verify zero npm/npx across all 4 repos.

discovery.approval_evidence: Fast-track. Agreement approved with proposal; contract minted (9 items, minimal). bun equivalences: npx playwright→bunx playwright, npx tsx→bun x tsx, npm run→bun run. No call site genuinely needs npm.

design.approval_evidence: Mechanical tool-swap (npm/npx→bun) in web + pokeedge dev scripts; no external surface. Execution = cross-project PRs. bunx playwright / bun x tsx / bun run equivalences confirmed. No validator needed.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved fast-track plan. T1 web PR (npm/npx→bun), T2 pokeedge PR (2 dev scripts→bun), T3 verify zero npm/npx all repos. AC1-3.

execution.approval_evidence: All 3 tasks checkpointed. Executable + high-value npm/npx eliminated: web (PRs #96 CI-path, #97 scripts+AGENTS+CONTRIBUTING+test-headers+ci-spec), pokeedge (#281 scripts). Long-tail prose + legit refs excluded per scope. PRs auto-merge armed.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User accepted with 'accept'. Contract 9/9. Executable + high-value npm/npx eliminated (PRs #96/#97/#281); long-tail prose + legit refs deferred/preserved per scope. Real defense tracked as enforceLifecycleScriptBlocking. Exec summary persisted.

origin.source_artifact: user directive: do not use npm (the tool) system-wide; audit found npm/npx in pokeedge-web + pokeedge

items.text: **AC1** pokeedge-web: zero `npm`/`npx` in executable/high-value surfaces — `package.json`, all `scripts/`, `AGENTS.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, test-file `Run with:` headers (`*.integration.ts`, `*.live-auth.ts`), `playwright.config*.ts`, and `docs/specs/ci-workflows.md` (spec flipped to forbid npm). Replaced with bun. (PRs #96 CI-path, #97 scripts+docs.)

**AC3** Verification: zero `npm`/`npx` in the executable + high-value surfaces above across the repos. Long-tail human-doc prose (TESTING.md tables, MAINTAINING_API_DOCS, runbooks, etc.) is **intentionally deferred** (docs don't execute; ~zero security value). Legit refs excluded: `Dockerfile` `npm install -g bun` (bun bootstrap), `repo-improve-prep.md` `npm audit` (historical reporting), advance `SETUP.md` (`npm install -g pnpm` option) + `slop-smells.yaml` (`npm audit` detection rule) + `acp-mux/` (separate package, deferred) + `benchmark-temporal.ts` usage comment (trivial).
Replace with each repo's standard tool (web → bun); no new package manager.
Do NOT corrupt legit references (bun bootstrap, npm-audit reporting, pnpm-install option, slop detection rule).
Do not introduce npm/npx in executable/high-value surfaces.
Do not change a repo's package manager.
Do not blind-sweep prose that breaks legit content.

pokeedge run_live.sh + sync_frontend.sh npm run→bun run (#281); rg zero npm/npx in pokeedge scripts.

documents.proposal:
## Why
npm runs lifecycle scripts by default (supply-chain risk); uv/bun/pnpm don't. Eliminating npm/npx is the clean, low-tradeoff supply-chain lever (no cooldown/allowlist machinery). Audit found npm/npx only in pokeedge-web (~18) + pokeedge (2 dev scripts); security-gates + advance are already clean.

## What Changes (cross-repo cleanup, no guard)
1. **pokeedge-web** (PR): replace all `npm`/`npx` with bun — `package.json` `test:e2e:prod-smoke` `npx playwright` → `bunx playwright`; dev scripts `npm run …` → `bun run …`, `npx tsx …` → `bun x tsx …`; update help-text strings to bun. (CI-path `npm version` already fixed in #96.)
2. **pokeedge** (PR): `run_live.sh` + `sync_frontend.sh` `npm run …` (frontend) → `bun run …`.
3. Confirm advance + security-gates remain clean.

## Success Criteria
- `rg -e '\\bnpm (ci|install|run|version|publish|audit|exec)\\b' -e '\\bnpx \\b'` across all four repos (excluding node_modules/lockfiles) returns **zero** invocations.
- Web + pokeedge dev scripts/tests run via bun; no behavior regression.
- No guard added (per decision) — this is a one-time sweep; npm-discipline is by convention + the existing advance lockfile-policy guard.

## Constraints
- Replace with each repo's standard tool (web → bun); do not introduce a different PM.
- Preserve script behavior (flags, args).
- Help-text/doc strings updated too (don't instruct users to run npm).

## Discovery Agenda
- Confirm `bunx` / `bun x` / `bun run` equivalences for each call site (playwright, tsx).
- Any call site that genuinely needs npm (none expected) — flag if found.

## Scope
### In Scope
pokeedge-web npm/npx → bun (scripts, package.json, help text); pokeedge 2 dev scripts → bun; verification sweep.
### Out of Scope
A CI "no-npm" guard (declined); the promote-to-staging CI fix (#96, already done); changing package managers; advance/security-gates (already clean).
### Must Not
Introduce npm/npx anywhere; change a repo's package manager; break dev-script behavior.
documents.problemStatement:
npm (the tool) runs dependency lifecycle scripts by default — the #1 supply-chain attack vector — whereas the package managers Sharper Flow actually standardizes on (uv for Python, bun for web, pnpm for advance) block lifecycle scripts by default. So the simplest, highest-leverage supply-chain win is to not invoke npm/npx anywhere.

Audit (system-wide):
- security-gates: clean. advance: clean (pnpm-disciplined + a lockfile-policy guard).
- pokeedge-web: ~18 npm/npx invocations — `package.json` `test:e2e:prod-smoke` uses `npx playwright`, and dev scripts use `npm run`/`npx tsx` (dev-stack.sh, refresh-auth, setup-mcp, mcp-test, analyze-flakes, help text). (The CI-path `npm version` in promote-to-staging.yml was hotfixed separately in PR #96.)
- pokeedge: 2 dev scripts call the web via `npm run` (`run_live.sh`, `sync_frontend.sh`).

Desired outcome: zero `npm`/`npx` invocations across the Sharper Flow repos — replaced with the repo's standard tool (bun for web, bun for the web-facing pokeedge dev scripts). No npm, system-wide.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement — eliminateNpmUsage (scope: executable + high-value docs)

Scope refined after the full audit revealed 100+ npm/npx references, mostly low-value prose with judgment cases that break under blind sed. Per decision: cover **executable + high-value/copy-pasted surfaces**, skip long-tail prose, exclude legit cases.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **AC1** pokeedge-web: zero `npm`/`npx` in executable/high-value surfaces — `package.json`, all `scripts/`, `AGENTS.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, test-file `Run with:` headers (`*.integration.ts`, `*.live-auth.ts`), `playwright.config*.ts`, and `docs/specs/ci-workflows.md` (spec flipped to forbid npm). Replaced with bun. (PRs #96 CI-path, #97 scripts+docs.)
- **AC2** pokeedge: `scripts/run_live.sh` + `scripts/sync_frontend.sh` `npm run`→`bun run`. (PR #281.)
- **AC3** Verification: zero `npm`/`npx` in the executable + high-value surfaces above across the repos. Long-tail human-doc prose (TESTING.md tables, MAINTAINING_API_DOCS, runbooks, etc.) is **intentionally deferred** (docs don't execute; ~zero security value). Legit refs excluded: `Dockerfile` `npm install -g bun` (bun bootstrap), `repo-improve-prep.md` `npm audit` (historical reporting), advance `SETUP.md` (`npm install -g pnpm` option) + `slop-smells.yaml` (`npm audit` detection rule) + `acp-mux/` (separate package, deferred) + `benchmark-temporal.ts` usage comment (trivial).

## Constraints
- Replace with each repo's standard tool (web → bun); no new package manager.
- Preserve script behavior.
- Do NOT corrupt legit references (bun bootstrap, npm-audit reporting, pnpm-install option, slop detection rule).

## Avoidances
- Do not introduce npm/npx in executable/high-value surfaces.
- Do not change a repo's package manager.
- Do not blind-sweep prose that breaks legit content.

## Notes
- CI-path `npm version` hotfixed in #96.
- The actual supply-chain defense is tracked separately as `enforceLifecycleScriptBlocking`.
documents.design:
# Design — eliminateNpmUsage

Mechanical cleanup; no external surfaces. Execution = cross-project PRs (web + pokeedge), plain git (no ADV checkpoint cross-project).

## 1. pokeedge-web PR (AC1)
- `package.json`: `test:e2e:prod-smoke` `npx playwright …` → `bunx playwright …`.
- dev scripts: `npm run X` → `bun run X`; `npx tsx Y` → `bun x tsx Y` (dev-stack.sh, mcp-test.sh, setup-mcp.sh, refresh-auth.ts, analyze-flake-rate.ts, curate-artist-bios.ts, download-pokemon-sprites.ts, lib/flake-analysis.ts help/usage strings + real invocations).
- Preserve flags/args exactly. Verify dev-stack.sh `bun run dev` works (vite via bun).

## 2. pokeedge PR (AC2)
- `scripts/run_live.sh:60` `npm run dev` → `bun run dev` (frontend is bun).
- `scripts/sync_frontend.sh:35` `npm run generate:api` → `bun run generate:api`.

## 3. Verify (AC3)
- `rg -e '\bnpm (ci|install|run|version|publish|audit|exec)\b' -e '\bnpx \b'` across all 4 repos (excl node_modules/lockfiles) → zero.

## Notes
- CI-path promote-to-staging `npm version` already hotfixed (#96) — out of scope here.
- Replacements are tool-swaps only; no logic change.

## Risk
- `bun x` vs `bunx`: both work; use `bunx` for the package.json playwright script (concise), `bun x` acceptable in shell scripts. Confirm `bun x tsx` resolves tsx (web has tsx dep).
documents.executiveSummary: Eliminated npm/npx from the executable and high-value surfaces across the Sharper Flow repos, choosing "don't use npm" as a clean supply-chain lever (npm runs dependency lifecycle scripts by default; bun/pnpm/uv block them). Audit found the footprint was much larger than first scoped (~100+ references, mostly low-value prose with judgment cases that would corrupt under blind sed), so the scope was deliberately narrowed to what actually runs or gets copy-pasted: pokeedge-web package.json + all scripts + AGENTS.md + CONTRIBUTING.md + test-file `Run with:` headers + playwright configs + the `docs/specs/ci-workflows.md` spec (flipped to forbid npm), and pokeedge's two frontend dev scripts. Delivered via PRs #96 (CI-path `npm version` hotfix — a regression I had introduced in the tag-only #93), #97 (web scripts + high-value docs), and #281 (pokeedge scripts); all auto-merge-armed. security-gates + the advance plugin were already npm-clean. Long-tail human-doc prose (TESTING.md tables, runbooks, MAINTAINING_API_DOCS, etc.) was intentionally deferred — docs don't execute and the churn/corruption risk outweighed ~zero security value — and genuinely-legitimate references were preserved untouched: the Dockerfile's `npm install -g bun` (bun bootstrap), historical `npm audit` findings reports, the `npm install -g pnpm` install option, the slop-smells `npm audit` detection rule, and the acp-mux package (separate, deferred). Crucially, this change is hygiene/defense-in-depth, not the primary attack defense — that is tracked separately as `enforceLifecycleScriptBlocking` (enforce script-blocking + frozen installs org-wide). Contract 9/9.
